evidence: Statement asks for signed sums of unit fractions delta_k/k with 0 < |sum| < c/2^n; erdosproblems.com/317 is tagged 'unit fractions'.
Variant bounds nonzero signed sums of unit fractions delta_k/k below by 1/lcm(1..n).
Property P1 concerns vanishing finite sums of signed unit fractions f_n/n; erdosproblems.com/318 is tagged 'unit fractions'.
Asks whether the squares excluding 1 admit a vanishing signed sum of unit fractions (property P1), solved by Larsen [La26].
Docstring: 'Every infinite arithmetic progression has property P1. This is proved in [Sa82b].'
Asks for the largest A in {1..N} whose signed unit-fraction sum vanishes with no vanishing proper subsum; erdosproblems.com/319 is tagged 'unit fractions'.
Asks for an additive complement A to the primes with |A cap {1..N}| = o((log N)^2), i.e. every large n is p + a with p prime.
Ruzsa's bound states liminf |A cap {1..N}|/log N >= e^gamma where gamma is the Euler-Mascheroni constant.
R(N) is the largest A in {1..N} with all subset sums of unit fractions 1/n distinct; erdosproblems.com/321 is tagged 'unit fractions'.
The cited Bleicher-Erdos bounds [BlEr76b] come from 'Denominators of Egyptian fractions. II'.
Docstring: a positive answer 'would have significant applications to Waring's problem'; f_{k,m} counts sums of m nonnegative k-th powers.
Asks for a polynomial f in Z[x] with all sums f(a)+f(b), a<b, distinct - i.e. whose values form a Sidon (B_2) set.
The k=3 case counts integers that are sums of three cubes; the cited best bound is Wooley [Wo15], 'Sums of three cubes, II'.
Docstring: 'Let A be an additive basis of order 2. Must there exist B ... which is also a basis such that lim b_k/k^2 does not exist?'
